text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Egg yolks and whole eggs store significant amounts of protein and choline.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Egg yolks and whole eggs store significant amounts of 15%","entailment":"The quote indicates that lightly fried eggs retain 5‑15% less vitamin B12 compared with the raw egg.","population":"","intervention":"lightly fried eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"vitamin B12 loss","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"5 - 15%","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Hard‑boiled eggs lose up to 20% of vitamin than twice that from scrambled eggs","entailment":"The sentence reports a comparative absorption rate, indicating higher bioavailability from fried or boiled eggs versus scrambled eggs.","population":"","intervention":"fried or boiled eggs","comparator":"scrambled eggs","outcome":"vitamin B12 absorption","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"more than twice","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Boiled eggs supply vitamin A at 19% of the Daily Value per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"vitamin A (19% DV)","entailment":"The quoted fragment lists vitamin A content of boiled eggs as 19% of the Daily Value per 100 g.","population":"","intervention":"boiled eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"vitamin A laying hens also may affect the nutritional quality of eggs.","entailment":"The quote directly suggests that what hens eat can influence egg nutrition.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"Chicken eggs that are especially high in omega‑3 fatty acids are produced by feeding hens a diet containing polyunsaturated fats from fish oil, chia seeds, or flaxseeds.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"chicken eggs that are especially high in omega-3 fatty acids are produced by feeding hens a diet containing polyunsaturated fats from sources such as fish oil, chia seeds, or flaxseeds","entailment":"The sentence links a hen diet rich in certain polyunsaturated fats to the production of omega‑3‑rich eggs.","population":"","intervention":"feeding hens a diet containing polyunsaturated fats from fish oil, chia seeds, or flaxseeds","comparator":"","outcome":"egg omega‑3 fatty acid content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Pasture‑raised free‑range hens produce eggs that are relatively enriched in omega‑3 fatty acids compared to those of cage‑raised chickens.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Pasture-raised free-range hens ... also produce eggs that are relatively enriched in enrichment in the former.","population":"","intervention":"pasture‑raised free‑range hens","comparator":"cage‑raised chickens","outcome":"egg omega‑3 enrichment","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"relatively enriched","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A 2010 USDA study determined there were no significant differences of macronutrients in various study’s finding that macronutrient content does not vary significantly among different eggs.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"macronutrient differences","timeframe":"2010","quantifiers":"no significant differences","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Cooked eggs are easier to digest than raw eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Cooked eggs are easier to digest than raw eggs","entailment":"The sentence directly compares digestibility, stating cooked eggs are more digestible than raw eggs.","population":"","intervention":"cooked eggs","comparator":"raw eggs","outcome":"digestibility","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Cooked eggs have a lower risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"More than half the calories found in eggs come from the fat in the yolk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"More than
